domain:
In the context of a theater organization, various plays are performed in one or more theaters, each identified by its own location, for example "The Northwest Theater". A play may have multiple scheduled sessions, and each session occurs on a scheduled date. Plays are identified by a unique name and is either a musical or an opera. Furthermore, each play may take place during the day or at night.

Each play is written by one or more authors, who are uniquely identified by name. Plays involve participants each also identified by name, and the same participant may act in multiple plays. For each play session, the starting time and an ending time is recorded, and participants can act in one or more sessions. Both authors and participant can be the same person, who may write a play and participate in its performance.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Play in the relationship with AuthorParticipant?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an author participant can exist without any associated play.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an author participant must have at least one associated play.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, AuthorParticipant can exist without Play.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, AuthorParticipant must have at least one Play.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, AuthorParticipant can exist without Play.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Play in the relationship with AuthorParticipant?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, an author participant is associated with exactly one play.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, an author participant can be associated with multiple plays.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each author participant links to one play.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each author participant can link to multiple plays.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each author participant can link to multiple plays.

questions.question:
We know that each theater has a name. When we think about name, is it just a simple piece of information, like a line of text? 
Or is name made up of different parts that might be important?
For example:
1. For each name, the detail information is saved separately, so users can filter theater by detail.
2. The name is a single piece of text. This works well when we just need to show the name.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The Name is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Model name as a simple representation in Theater.
questions.selected: Option 2: A single line of text for name is enough.

questions.question:
When thinking about the name of theaters, are there only specific, predefined values it can have, or can it be any text?
For example:
1. The name can be any text value that users want to enter.
2. The name must be one of these specific values: the northwest theater.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for name.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Enumeration name with predefined values: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: The name can be any text value.
